volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 2.2.232 - Section 2.3.317)

sections.label: Section 2.2.232
sections.original: Di immortales ! homini homo quid praestat , stulto intelligens
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! how much does one man excel another ! What a difference there is between a wise person and a fool ! This strongly came into my mind from the following circumstance . As I was , coming along to-day , I met a certain person of this place , of my own rank and station ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.235
sections.original: Hominem haud impurum , itidem patria qui abligurierat bona .
sections.translation: no mean fellow , one who , like myself , had guttled away his paternal estate ; I saw him , shabby , dirty , sickly , beset with rags and years ;— What ’ s the meaning of this garb ? said I ; he answered , Because , wretch that I am , I ’ ve lost what I possessed : see to what I am reduced ,— all my acquaintances and friends forsake me . On this I felt contempt for him in : comparison with myself . What ! said I , you pitiful sluggard ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.240
sections.original: Itan parasti te , ut spes nulla reliqua in te sit tibi ?
sections.translation: have you so managed matters as to have no hope left ? Have you lost your wits together with your estate ? Don ’ t you see me , who have risen from the same condition ? What a complexion I have , how spruce and well dressed , what portliness of person ? I have every thing , yet have nothing ; and although I possess nothing , still , of nothing am I in want . But I , said he , unhappily , can neither be a butt nor submit to blows . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.245
sections.original: Possum . Quid ? tu his rebus credis fieri ? tota erras via .
sections.translation: What ! said I , do you suppose it is managed by those means ? You are quite mistaken . Once upon a time , in the early ages , there was a calling for that class ; this is a new mode of coney-catching ; I , in fact , have been the first to strike into this path . There is a class of men who strive to be the first in every thing , but are not ; to these I make my court ; I do not present myself to them to be laughed at ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.250
sections.original: Sed his ultro arrideo , et eorum ingenia admiror simul .
sections.translation: but I am the first to laugh with them , and at the same time to admire their parts : whatever they say , I commend ; if they contradict that self-same thing , I commend again . Does any one deny ? I deny : does he affirm ? I affirm : in fine , I have so trained myself as to humor them in every thing . This calling is now by far the most productive .

sections.label: Section 2.2.254
sections.original: Scitum hercle hominem ! hic homines prorsum ex stultis insanos facit .
sections.translation: A clever fellow , upon my faith ! From being fools he makes men mad outright .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255
sections.original: Dum haec loquimur interea loci ad macellum ubi advenimus ,
sections.translation: While we were thus talking , in the mean time we arrived at the market-place ; overjoyed , all the confectioners ran at once to meet me ; fishmongers , [*] butchers , cooks , [*] sausage-makers , and fishermen , whom , both when my fortunes were flourishing and when they were ruined , I had served , and often serve still : they complimented me , asked me to dinner , and gave me a hearty welcome .

sections.label: Section 2.2.260
sections.original: Ille ubi miser famelicus videt me esse in tanto honore ,
sections.translation: When this poor hungry wretch saw that I was in such great esteem , and that I obtained a living so easily , then the fellow began to entreat me that I would allow him to learn this method of me ; I bade him become my follower [*] if he could ; as the disciples of the Philosophers take their names from the Philosophers themselves , so too , the Parasite ; ought to be called Gnathonics .

sections.label: Section 2.2.265
sections.original: Viden otium et cibus quid faciat alienus ?
sections.translation: Do you see the effects of ease and feeding at another ’ s cost ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.265b
sections.translation: But why do I delay to take this girl to Thais , and ask her to come to dinner ? ( Aside , on seeing PARMENO .) But I see Parmeno , our rival ’ s servant , waiting before the door of Thais with a sorrowful air ; all ’ s safe ; no doubt these people are finding a cold welcome . I ’ m resolved to have some sport with this knave .

sections.label: Section 2.2.269b
sections.translation: They fancy that , through this present ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.270
sections.translation: Thais is quite their own .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270b
sections.translation: With his very best wishes Gnatho greets Parmeno , his very good friend .— What are you doing ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.271b
sections.translation: I ’ m standing . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.271c
sections.translation: So I perceive . Pray , do you see any thing here that don ’ t please you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.272b
sections.translation: Yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.272c
sections.translation: I believe you ,— but any thing else , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.273
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.273b
sections.translation: Because you are out of spirits .

sections.label: Section 2.2.273c
sections.translation: Not in the least .

sections.label: Section 2.2.273d
sections.translation: Well , don ’ t be so ; but what think you of this slave ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.274b
sections.translation: Really , not amiss .

sections.label: Section 2.2.274c
sections.translation: I ’ ve galled the fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.2.274d
sections.translation: How mistaken you are in your notion !

sections.label: Section 2.2.275
sections.original: Quam hoc munus gratum Thaidi arbitrare esse ?
sections.translation: How far do you suppose this gift will prove acceptable to Thais ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.275b
sections.translation: It ’ s this you mean to say now , that we are discarded there . Hark you , there are vicissitudes in all things .

sections.label: Section 2.2.277
sections.original: Sex ego te totos Parmeno hos menses quietum reddam ,
sections.translation: For the next six months , Parmeno , I ’ ll set you at ease ; you sha ’ n ’ t have to be running to and fro , or sitting up till daylight . Don ’ t I make you happy ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.279b
sections.translation: Me ? O prodigiously !

sections.label: Section 2.2.279c
sections.translation: That ’ s my way with my friends .

sections.label: Section 2.2.279d
sections.translation: I commend you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.280
sections.original: Detineo te fortasse : tu profectus alio fueras .
sections.translation: I ’ m detaining you ; perhaps you were about to go somewhere else .

sections.label: Section 2.2.281
sections.translation: Nowhere .

sections.label: Section 2.2.281b
sections.original: Tum tu igitur paululum da mihi operae : fac ut admittar
sections.translation: In that case then , lend me your services a little ; let me be introduced to her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.282b
sections.original: Age modo ; nunc tibi patent fores hae , quia istam ducis .
sections.translation: Very well ; ( GNATHO knocks at the door , which immediately opens ) now the door is open for you , ( aside ) because you are bringing her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.283
sections.translation: Should you like any one to be called out from here ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.283b
sections.translation: Only let the next two days go by ; you who , at present , in such high favor , are opening the door with one little finger ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.285
sections.original: Nae tu istas faxo calcibus saepe insultabis frustra .
sections.translation: assuredly I ’ ll cause to be kicking at that door full oft , with your heels , to no purpose .

sections.label: Section 2.2.286
sections.original: Etiam nunc hic stas Parmeno ? eho numnam hic relictus custos ,
sections.translation: Still standing here , Parmeno ? Why now , have you been left on guard here , that no go-between might perchance be secretly running from the Captain to her ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.288
sections.original: Facete dictum : mira vero militi quae placeant .
sections.translation: Smartly said ; really they ought to be wonderful things to please the Captain . But I see my master ’ s youngest son coming this way ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.290
sections.original: Miror quid ex Piraeeo abierit ; nam ibi custos publice est nunc .
sections.translation: I wonder why he has come away from the Piraeus , [*] for he is at present on guard there in the public service . It ’ s not for nothing ; he ’ s coming in a hurry , too ; I can ’ t imagine why he ’ s looking around in all directions .

sections.label: Section 2.3.292
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone ! The girl is nowhere ; nor do I know where I am myself , to have lost sight of her . Where to inquire for her , where to search for her , whom to ask , which way to turn ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.295
sections.original: Incertus sum : una haec spes est ; ubi ubi est , diu celari non potest .
sections.translation: I ’ m at a loss . I have only this hope ; wherever she is , she can not long be concealed . O what beauteous features ! from this moment I banish all other women from my thoughts ; I can not endure these every-day beauties .

sections.label: Section 2.3.297b
sections.translation: Why look , here ’ s the other one . He ’ s saying something , I don ’ t know what , about love .

sections.label: Section 2.3.298b
sections.translation: O unfortunate old man , their father ! This assuredly is a youth , who , if he does begin , you will say that

sections.label: Section 2.3.300
sections.original: Illum alterum fuisse praeut quae dabit huius rabies .
sections.translation: the other one was mere play and pastime , compared with what the madness of this one will cause .

sections.label: Section 2.3.301
sections.original: Ut illum Di Deaeque senium perdant qui me hodie remoratus est ,
sections.translation: May all the Gods and Goddesses confound that old fellow who detained me to-day , and me as well who stopped for him , and in fact troubled myself a straw about him . But see , here ’ s Parmeno . ( Addressing him .) Good-morrow to you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.303b
sections.original: Quid tu es tristis ? quidve es alacris ?
sections.translation: Why are you out of spirits , and why in such a hurry ? Whence come you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.304b
sections.original: Egone ? nescio hercle , neque unde eam , neque quorsumn eam :
sections.translation: What , I ? I ’ faith , I neither know whence I ’ m come , nor whither I ’ m going ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.305
sections.translation: so utterly have I lost myself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.306
sections.translation: How , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.306b
sections.translation: I ’ m in love .

sections.label: Section 2.3.306c
sections.translation: Ha !

sections.label: Section 2.3.306d
sections.original: Nunc Parmeno te ostendes qui vir sies .
sections.translation: Now , Parmeno , you may show what sort of a man you are . You know that you often promised me to this effect : Chaerea , do you only find some object to fall in love with ; I ’ ll make you sensible of my usefulness in such matters , when I used to be storing up my father ’ s provisions for you on the sly in your little room . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.310
sections.translation: To the point , you simpleton .

sections.label: Section 2.3.310b
sections.original: Hoc hercle factum est : fac sis nunc promissa appareant ;
sections.translation: Upon my faith , this is the fact . Now , then , let your promises be made good , if you please , or if indeed the affair is a deserving one for you to exert your energies upon . The girl isn ’ t like our girls , whom their mothers are anxious to have with shoulders kept down , and chests well girthed , [*] that they may be slender . If one is a little inclined to plumpness , they declare that she ’ s training for a boxer , [*] and stint her food ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.315
sections.original: Tametsi bona est natura , reddunt curatura iunceas :
sections.translation: although their constitutions are good , by their treatment they make them as slight as bulrushes ; and so for that reason they are admired , forsooth .

sections.label: Section 2.3.316b
sections.translation: What sort of a girl is this one of yours ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.316c
sections.translation: A new style of beauty .

sections.label: Section 2.3.316d
sections.translation: Astounding !

sections.label: Section 2.3.317
sections.original: Color verus , corpus solidum et succi plenum .
sections.translation: Her complexion genuine , [*] her flesh firm and full of juiciness . [*]
